missing_info: What device/IP sent the 25 pings to 172.16.0.12 (source address and hostname/device)?
From that source device: provide the traceroute to 172.16.0.12 (or traceroute output).
On R2 (the subnet gateway for 172.16.0.0/24): outputs of 'show ip interface brief' and 'show interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0' to confirm interface state and errors.
On R2: 'show arp | include 172.16.0.12' (to see if ARP entry exists) and 'ping 172.16.0.12' run from R2 (reachability from the local subnet).

From the host 172.16.0.12 (or its administrator): confirm the host is powered on, its IP/netmask/gateway, and whether host firewall permits ICMP (or provide a local ping/ifconfig/ipconfig/iptables output).

evidence: Router2 ping to 172.16.0.12: "Sending 5... Success rate is 0 percent (0/5)" (call lc_328d8999-9cd8-474b-9978-6aa17f91c74c / lc_1c1ebf42-0b48-4087-b718-b7e6195bfb77)
R2 LAN interface up: GigabitEthernet0/0/0 admin_state="up", oper_state="up", IP configured as 172.16.0.1/24 (get_interface_status + R2 running-config)
Traceroute from R1 reaches R2 (10.0.0.2) as hop 1 then shows only '*' thereafter — failure occurs beyond R2 toward the 172.16.0.0/24 host (call lc_e3396450-dc94-46fd-8e0a-9b2a98bb9b74)
Router2 (directly on 172.16.0.0/24) receives no ICMP response: "Success rate is 0 percent (0/5)"

Router ACLs on R1 and R2 do not show a blanket deny for ICMP on the 172.16.0.0/24 network (R1_TO_R2 and R2_TO_R1 deny specific TCP ports then permit ip), so network-layer ACLs are unlikely to be blocking ICMP
Router2 is in the same IP subnet (172.16.0.1/24) and interface is up, yet pings to 172.16.0.12 fail (0/5) — consistent with no ARP reply or L2 reachability to that specific IP
Traceroute from R1 does not progress beyond R2, indicating failure toward the destination on the LAN side rather than on upstream links

missing_info: Show R2 ARP entry for 172.16.0.12 (e.g. 'show ip arp 172.16.0.12') to confirm whether R2 has an ARP entry or received any ARP reply.
Provide server-side status: is the server powered on and what is its interface IP/mask/gateway (ipconfig/ifconfig)?

If a switch is between R2 and the server, provide the switch interface status and MAC-table entry for the server's MAC (show interface status, show mac address-table) or the switch port connected to the server.

plan_history.intent_description: Check reachability and identify cause of server connectivity failure to 172.16.0.12 (25 pings sent, 0 successful).
plan_history.problem: Host 172.16.0.12 is unreachable from the network (25 ICMP pings sent, 0 replies).
plan_history.fix_summary: Gather diagnostic data from the subnet gateway (router2) and from the originating/source device (assumed router1) to determine whether the issue is local (interface/ARP/host) or upstream, then proceed with targeted remediation.

plan_history.intent_description: Check reachability and identify cause of server connectivity failure to 172.16.0.12 (25 pings sent, 0 successful).
plan_history.problem: Host 172.16.0.12 is not responding to ICMP from directly-connected router (R2). Likely host down, host ICMP-filtering, or L2/ARP failure on the LAN segment.
plan_history.fix_summary: Gather ARP/L2 evidence from R2 and clear stale ARP entry to force ARP re-resolution on the LAN segment so server reachability can be retested; if no ARP seen, escalate to server/switch admin for power/cabling and server firewall checks.

remaining_issues: 172.16.0.12 remains unreachable from the directly-connected router (ICMP probes from R2 show 0/5 success).
Cause still undetermined: could be host powered off / NIC down / cabling / switch-port problem (L2/ARP failure), or the host could be up but filtering ICMP (host firewall).
ARP/L2 evidence from R2 for 172.16.0.12 was not collected, so we cannot tell whether R2 ever learned the host's MAC or if ARP resolution is failing.
